title: Automated Sorting
excerpt: I would like request a feature to have the sort feature improved when you are in a sort view that any new items will automatically get sorted on the board without having to resave the board view. (i.e. - If you have a bo…

posts.author: KevinEntmaa (Kevin Entmaa)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like request a feature to have the sort feature improved when you are in a sort view that any new items will automatically get sorted on the board without having to resave the board view. (i.e. - If you have a board sorted by date, when a new item is created it would automatically put that item in the new correct order).

posts.author: Z.Swank (Zac Swank)
posts.cookedHtml: I second this request. In scanning other feature requests, there are several other threads that request this feature and this thread appears to indicate that the feature is available, but I doesn’t seem to work for me.

posts.author: mkingsland (Morgan Kingsland)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!

We have many boards that are sorted by due date, not by date a pulse was entered. Is it possible for the board to auto-sort by date any time a new pulse is added?

Example: I add a high urgency project to a board for our design team, but it automatically lives at the bottom of the group. I’d like this to move up in the queue without my having to click the sort option at the top of the column.

Thanks!

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
i would love this one!
i have made a request for this one!
@Julia-monday.com

this is in the roadmap?

posts.author: agranville (Andre Granville)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m eager to see this one in Monday also.

posts.author: stifoo (Stephanie Monty)
posts.cookedHtml: For now, If I needed that function I would make a date column for “assigned on” or something like that. Then you could easily sort by that.

posts.author: danhawkins (Dan Hawkins)
posts.cookedHtml: Would love this too. We have lots of boards where pulses are created by automations and integrations so it would be great if they could be auto sorted by our priority status column.

posts.author: Kibet (Kibet)
posts.cookedHtml: This is definitely a need! @Tom-monday.com any possibility this will be implemented soon? Thanks in advance.

posts.author: Adrian (Adrian F. Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml:
I requested this feature as well through email. Even though I used it more as a playful way into getting our teammates to use Monday.com I found it as an actual useful tool like you just mentioned @mkingsland

The way I initially used it was to have all our team mates vote for a restaurant they’d all like to eat at. I wanted the most voted items to be able to climb to the top and have them get excited.

  • I love that idea!
  • Is this what you are using Monday.com for??
0 voters

posts.author: jontc (Jonathan Thiesse)
posts.cookedHtml: Tweak… While an default/auto sort would be nice; to me, an even better feature would be a way to save a board’s table view based on the current sort. That way, the user isn’t stuck with one auto-sort view.

posts.author: Aaren (Aaren)
posts.cookedHtml: @jontc — That’d be a flexible implementation. I like this idea.

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @jontc we actually do have this functionality via the use of creating views! If you filter the board, you can save that to a new view and go to that view for the specific filter

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml:
As for the sorting of columns, this is something we are looking into everyone! We appreciate all of the feedback

You could filter the board by the Creation Log column for the time being!

posts.author: johana.Echelon (Johanna Izquierdo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Brett, Is there any update on this feature?

Thank you

posts.author: anthonywoods (Anthony Woods)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @brett-monday.com,

Do you have any updates or timeline on when the auto sort feature will implemented? We are new to Monday.com and we absolutely love the platform but with the nature of our marketing business we are constantly closing tasks when completed and within our completed board any new tasks that are added appear at the bottom.

We understand there is a sort feature which you can click on each time but I think this would be a “quality of life” adjustment for when we check our boards they are automatically sorted without the need of any manual interaction. It can become a pain if we are checking these boards more than once per day.

Thanks

posts.author: joshua.davison (Josh Davison)
posts.cookedHtml: I was about to submit a new feature request when I noticed this has already been suggested. One extra detail that I had is that the “auto sort” (or “persistent sort” as I was going to call it) should be saved against the views of the board - so you could auto-sort one view by one set of rules and another view by another set of rules.

posts.author: rick (Rick Castorani)
posts.cookedHtml:
It looks like there is a new Sort button but it is still only doing a one-time sort, even though the action item says “Save to board”. So the way it reads to me is that it seems like this functionality has been added but isn’t currently working?

Sort button:

Action text:

posts.author: joshua.davison (Josh Davison)
posts.cookedHtml: I also just noticed this appear, but have also noticed it does not appear to be working (if it is intended to be auto-sort). It does a manual sort, but new items being added and changed items are not sorted automatically, and it doesn’t “remember” what you sorted by so it doesn’t make it a 1 click sort either. I hope this is an auto-sort feature that’s just broken, rather than simply being a new interface to the same old manual sort we already had.

posts.author: anthonywoods (Anthony Woods)
posts.cookedHtml: With any luck this will be an auto pulse sort

posts.author: rick (Rick Castorani)
posts.cookedHtml: @brett-monday.com can you let us know if the new sort button will eventually have persistent/auto-sorting of the board? It looks like you can “save to table” a sort but once you save the new sort it disappears and reverts to a one-time sort. Is this the desired functionality of this button or will it be built out to actually save sorts?

posts.author: SueJ (Sue)
posts.cookedHtml: We too, would love the ability for the auto-sort for the same reason that others have shared. Will look forward to seeing this soon (fingers crossed).

posts.author: sean01844 (SEAN QUINN)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! This is a needed feature. If a teammate adds a pulse with a status of “Urgent” (vs. “Important” for example") I want that to automatically pop to the top of the group, not wait for me to re-sort the pulses to see it. Hoping this is in the woks and active soon!

posts.author: TomHaubner (Tom Haubner)
posts.cookedHtml: We also need this feature. One option to realize this might be to use the automation … “when prio changes sort column descending” . Unfortunately, the sort feature is not available via automation … but this might also be a way to realize this feature. Other apps do it like that.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve posted a more specific list of suggestions related to this post here: Bigger, Badder, More Better Sorting

posts.author: thusitha (Thusitha Manathunga)
posts.cookedHtml:
@sean01844 @SueJ
Hi all,
You can use 1View app to solve this issue.
Watch this demo to see how it works and with just 2 clicks you can add it to your monday account easily.
You can read more about this on here.

1View is still in its early stages and send your suggestions, feature requests and bug reports to support@serandibsoft.com.

posts.author: Mark_game1 (Mark Peterson)
posts.cookedHtml:
But the sort doesn’t save. If anything new is added you have to re-do the whole process of entering sorting fields.

It is really a simple ask and I can’t understand why something so simple would take so long.

posts.author: Dan_T (Dan Thomas)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’ve been trialing Monday recently and were just about to jump on board (and make the switch from Asana/Notion), but this lack of sorting feature is 100% a dealbreaker for us. There’s no way we can use Monday if we can’t automatically sort task lists by date.

I actually don’t understand what the ‘Save’ sort feature does? It seems like it does nothing at all?

The rest of the features and integrations/automations in Monday seem amazing, but it’s incredibly surprising that such a basic feature (that is available on literally every other project/task management platform) is missing from a system that has so many integration and automation options.

posts.author: ericgoetz (Eric Goetz)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed, @Dan_T. The “save sort” feature is incredibly misleading, if it does anything at all. It is a constant source of frustration, confusion, and miscommunication on my team. Really frustrating.

posts.author: mildorviolon (Jean-Francois McDonald)
posts.cookedHtml: You have my vote on this one, auto-sort is a must !

posts.author: GAP1969 (Gregory Pruitt)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a great feature to have! Any update from any Monday.com representative would be appreciated.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
This now appears to be happening!

Thank you monday!

(it would be nice if you let us know about changes like this.)

posts.author: joshua.davison (Josh Davison)
posts.cookedHtml: You’re right. It seems that the sort is automatically being applied whenever I access a view, but not automatically changing while I am within the view. Which I think is a really smart solution. Is that how it’s working for you?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
Mostly yes.

The coolest part is that if another user add/changes an item it will be inserted/moved to the proper place in the view you have immediately.

posts.author: stuttters (Jon Stuttfield)
posts.cookedHtml: This doesn’t appear to work in the Main Table view for some reason. The only way around this, as far as I can see, is to create a duplicate view and create the sort on that.

posts.author: mw01 (MW)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. I can’t seem to save on “Main Table” view either. Is this something that can happen?

posts.author: col-wthomas (Wade Thomas)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. I CANNOT believe this doesn’t work already. Having to sort every time defeats the purpose. I want upcoming pulses to bubble to the top. Not be mixed in among others with random due dates

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s great that it works for the main items now, but the fact that I can’t do this for subitems is really frustrating. We need to have the subitems auto-sort whenever a new task gets added.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: I think its not totally working for main items, i can set the order criteria, it automatically sort the items when added, but when i go out the main view, and go back, the sort configuration i have previously set is not there, and the only options to keep it saved is creating a new view, and i dont want it.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hlopezvc:

ly working for main items, i can set the order criteria, it automatically sort the items when added, but when i go out the main view, and go back, the sort configuration i have previously set is not there, and the only options to keep it saved is creating a new view, and i dont want it.

You have to save to sort in a new view. When adding new items they are sorted. When changing view to main (not sorted) and back to the sorted view the items are still sorted. See this Loom: https://www.loom.com/share/68df3b0e659049af85e0399ec7ae0a0f

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on auto-sort for subitems? Our team still desperately needs that and I would think most people using subitems as a task list would benefit from it.

posts.author: KTPM (Michael Fitzgerald)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates here? This bug is causing real-world issues. We just had a high profile client very upset because a task was missed due to the lack of auto-sort for newly created items. As described at the start of this discussion back in July 2019, when an item is created via automation, it is placed at the bottom of the group, despite an auto-sort by date being set & saved on the custom view. Also, sort by date does not work when multiple boards are linked.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @KTPM,

The item (created via the automation) should sort correctly if you saved the sort in a new view. The item will not automatically sort in the main table, as any sort or filter is saved to a new table view. If this isn’t the case on your end, I’d recommend reaching out to support@monday.com to investigate this

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Following on from @basdebruin, you also have the option to save your sorted view as the default board view in-case this helps:

Screen Shot 2022-11-14 at 12.00.01 pm750×386 33.5 KB

posts.author: jmaru (Maru Quinsaat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Automatically sort items by default based on a Date Column. (Newest /Oldest)

This way users wont have to toggle sort in a date column each time a new item is created.

posts.author: kaylak (Kayla K)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jmaru ,

If you sort the table by date → click on the “Sort” near the top of the screen → Click “Save to this View” it will automatically move items by the date you chose to sort.

If you want to have this is as the default view, you can hover over the newly created group → Click the 3 dots → Set as board default.

Let me know if you have any questions, hope this helps!

posts.author: jmaru (Maru Quinsaat)
posts.cookedHtml: My apologies in advance, I have no clue where to go for the instructions you just mentioned. I have no dedicated sort button if that makes sense. I can hover over a date column and manually sort the items by date. My items move from one group to another as they complete certain tasks. It doesn’t get sorted automatically based on the date column mentioned everytime it moves. So each time an item is moved I have to manually sort that new group it went into. I hope I’m making sense. Thank you for the advise though.

posts.author: kaylak (Kayla K)
posts.cookedHtml:
@jmaru ,

Do you see this “Sort” button highlighted in blue in the picture below on your board?
